title: Day 09 Interview Prep
description: Practice debouncing, custom hooks, and controlled component patterns from today's roadmap.

questions.question: What is debouncing and why use it in search?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Practical optimization knowledge, not just definition.
keyPoints: Delays action until input stops changing
Reduces API calls and server load
questions.sampleAnswer: Debouncing waits until the user stops typing before firing an action. In search, typing 'batman' without debounce sends six API calls. With 400ms debounce, one call fires after the last keystroke. This reduces server load, saves bandwidth, and prevents race conditions from overlapping requests.
followUps: What delay would you pick and why?

questions.question: What are the rules for custom hooks in React?
keyPoints: Same rules of hooks as components
questions.sampleAnswer: Custom hooks must start with use and can call other hooks like useState and useEffect. They follow the same rules of hooks — only call at top level, only from React functions. I extract useDebounce when debounce logic is reused or when the component gets too large.

questions.question: What is a controlled component in the context of search?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Week 1 foundation applied to search UIs.
keyPoints: React state is single source of truth
Input reflects state, state updates on change
Enables debounce layer between input and fetch
questions.sampleAnswer: A controlled search input stores the term in useState. The input value={search} and onChange updates state. React owns the value — not the DOM. This lets me show instant UI feedback on every keystroke while debouncing the value that triggers fetch.

questions.question: Implement useDebounce verbally — what state, effects, and cleanup do you need?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Can they write the hook from memory?
questions.sampleAnswer: I keep debouncedValue in useState initialized to the input value. useEffect watches value and delay. Inside, setTimeout updates debouncedValue after delay ms. Return cleanup that clearTimeout. Export debouncedValue. Fetch effect depends on debouncedValue, not raw search.

questions.question: How do you type a paginated movie search API response in TypeScript?
keyPoints: Separate API types from UI view models if needed
questions.sampleAnswer: I define PaginatedResponse<T> with page, total_pages, total_results, and results: T[]. Movie has id, title, poster_path, release_date. MovieSearchResponse is PaginatedResponse<Movie>. fetchMovies returns Promise<MovieSearchResponse>. Components map over results typed as Movie[].

questions.question: User searches 'star' then quickly 'star wars'. Old 'star' results appear briefly. How do you fix it?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Race condition handling from Day 08 applied to search.
keyPoints: Stale response from slower first request
Ignore responses that do not match current query
questions.sampleAnswer: This is a race condition. The 'star' request may resolve after 'star wars'. I use AbortController in useEffect cleanup to cancel in-flight fetch, or a cancelled flag. I also clear movies when debouncedSearch changes so stale data never displays.

questions.question: Explain how setTimeout relates to the JavaScript event loop at a high level.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Event loop intro required by roadmap — depth comes Day 11.
questions.sampleAnswer: JavaScript runs one call stack. setTimeout does not run immediately — it schedules a callback as a macrotask. After current synchronous code and microtasks finish, the event loop picks up the timer callback. Debounce relies on this — each clearTimeout cancels a pending macrotask.

questions.question: A teammate puts fetch directly in onChange for instant results. Review their PR — what do you say?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Code review communication and production awareness.
questions.sampleAnswer: I open Network tab and show six requests for six letters — unsustainable at scale and causes race conditions. I suggest controlled input for instant UI, useDebounce for API trigger, fetch in useEffect on debouncedSearch. I pair on the refactor and add a note about typical 400ms delay.
redFlags: Instant fetch is always better UX
